reasoning: The Biden administration's January 2025 export controls on advanced AI chips and model weights were heavily criticized and rescinded by the incoming Trump administration right before their May 15, 2025 compliance deadline. Instead of implementing "new or expanded export controls," the current administration fundamentally rolled them back. While BIS issued a "Policy Statement" and some due diligence/red flag guidance specifically targeting "countries of concern" (like China), these merely clarify the enforcement of existing rules rather than creating "specific controls on cloud-based access" or expanding global controls. The overarching criteria require the implementation of "new or expanded export controls"; a rescission and shift toward general compliance guidance are the opposite of this. Given the policy trajectory and with only 7 weeks remaining between the November 9, 2025 knowledge cutoff and the end of the year, there is virtually no chance the administration abruptly reverses course and implements new, broad restrictions.
batch_queries: Will the United States implement new or expanded export controls on advanced AI chips, cloud access to AI computing, and/or AI model weights in 2025?
